newsSince: Broadcom reported record Q2 FY2026 results (June 3, just before the window) with AI semiconductor revenue more than doubling to $10.8B; management guided Q3 consolidated revenue +84% YoY to ~$29.4B and AI revenue tripling to ~$16B, and reiterated >$100B FY2027 AI-revenue guidance across six custom-ASIC customers (Anthropic, Google, Meta, OpenAI, plus two). The post-earnings de-rating left the stock ~20% off highs with the thesis accelerating.

sources.label: Broadcom AI chip revenue doubled, $100B in 2027 (TIKR)

verdicts.rankRationale: On the residual-alpha-vs-passives lens nothing changed since June 9: a $1.8T benchmark mega-cap cannot become under-owned or hidden. No new catalyst; the post-earnings facts were already priced into the June 9 hold. Stays a monitor/benchmark name.
verdicts.neutral: Real AI exposure but it is the index, not residual alpha vs passives.
verdicts.bear: Mega-cap, widely owned, far from the passive/power bottleneck.

verdicts.rankRationale: On the AI-node-bottleneck lens the thesis is intact and accelerating: custom ASIC + scale-up Ethernet revenue tripling to ~$16B in Q3 (+~200% y/y) and >$100B FY27 AI target reiterated, while the stock de-rated post-earnings. Sell-off with thesis strengthening improves node-relevance asymmetry; continue up.
verdicts.bull: Primary custom-ASIC design partner to six hyperscalers; AI revenue tripling to $16B and >$100B FY27 target.
verdicts.neutral: High market cap and visibility cap the early-alpha score even as the node thesis accelerates.
verdicts.bear: Heavy customer concentration; 'chips-only' shift narrows system-level capture; lofty FY27 number must be hit.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Custom XPU/ASIC and scale-up fabric ramp to $16B/qtr
verdicts.primaryRisk: Hyperscaler concentration; FY27 AI target execution

verdicts.rankRationale: The page's own thesis concedes AVGO's CPO alpha is 'not undiscovered'; nothing since June 9 makes the consensus ecosystem anchor under-owned vs smaller CPO pure-plays. -2.2% drift only. Hold the low rank on this crowding-sensitive lens.
verdicts.bull: CPO platform leader and ecosystem anchor for AI networking.
verdicts.neutral: Alpha comes from share gains, not from being undiscovered.
verdicts.bear: Large-cap valuation, customer concentration, consensus ownership.

verdicts.rankRationale: Already rank 1 on the silicon-consumption basket and the quarter validated it (AI silicon $10.8B to guided ~$16B/qtr). No new data since June 9; the de-rating changes price, not theme leadership. Cleanest dual silicon-consumption proxy (ASIC + interconnect); hold the top slot.
verdicts.bull: Two fast-growing silicon needs at once: custom accelerators + AI networking.
verdicts.neutral: Best basket proxy but already at rank 1, limited room to rise.
verdicts.bear: Mega-cap; any AI-capex air-pocket hits the proxy hardest.

verdicts.rankRationale: On the master bottleneck list the optics/high-speed-networking thesis is intact and accelerating (AI revenue doubling, guided to triple), and YTD is only +10% with the stock ~20% off highs after the post-earnings flush. The setup that drove June 9's up-move persists; continue up modestly.
verdicts.bull: Scale-out bandwidth/optics exposure accelerating; AI revenue tripling guidance; YTD reset to +10% improves entry.
verdicts.neutral: Mega-cap status limits the under-owned-bottleneck premium this list prizes.
verdicts.bear: Customer concentration and 'chips-only' shift; networking execution and pricing must hold.
